last_agent_result.summary: Root cause: thoughtChannelRegex pattern does not match actual thinking channel marker format (expects slashes `</|channel|>` but actual markers use `<|channel>thought\n` and `<channel|>` without slashes). Since the regex never matches, the extractText() function passes through raw channel text unchanged. Additionally, extractText() uses channels["text"] directly without validating content, and the fallback message.toString() may include unparsed channel structure data. Fix: update regex to match actual marker format and add content validation.
last_agent_result.problem_statement: Bot responses appear garbled/corrupted - users see mangled text that includes channel markers or missing content

evidence.detail: Regex '<\|channel\|>[\s\S]*?<\|channel\|>' expects opening '<|channel|>' and closing '</|channel|>' (with slashes)
getThinkingChannel() defines start='<|channel>thought\n' and end='<channel|>' - NO slashes, different format entirely
extractText() uses message.channels["text"]?.toString() ?: message.toString() - if channels["text"] is null or contains raw channel data, no validation occurs before display
extractText(message) is called in both sendMessageAgentic and sendMessageSingleTurn on every onMessage callback - garbled text would propagate directly to UI
thoughtChannelRegex and extractText() were ADDED (not pre-existing) - the garbled output may be a regression from this change

assumptions: The channels["text"] API returns raw text content without channel wrapper parsing
The model outputs thinking content between <|channel>thought\n and <channel|> markers which goes into channels["thought"]
The text channel may include its own markers <|channel|>text\n...<channel|> that are NOT being stripped by the current regex
No adb access means live logs cannot be retrieved to confirm exact garbled output format
acceptance_criteria: Bot responses display clean readable text without channel marker artifacts
Bot responses contain complete sentences and proper formatting
Thinking content (when enabled) appears only in the thinking bubble, not in the main text response
The extractText function returns non-null, non-empty string for valid messages
risks: Without adb access, cannot verify exact marker format from live model output - regex fix is based on code inspection only
The regex fix assumes text channel markers follow same pattern as thought channel - may not be true
Could introduce new regressions if extractText changes affect normal (non-thinking) responses
Model behavior may differ between GPU and NPU backends - testing on one backend may not catch issues on the other
test_plan: Build and install APK on device with NPU backend
Send a simple message (e.g., 'Hello, how are you?') and observe response
Send a message that triggers extended thinking and verify thinking content is separate from main text
Send a PDF for processing and verify the response text is readable (not garbled)
Check on GPU backend as well to ensure text extraction works for both configurations
Log the raw channels["text"] value before and after extractText() to verify no artifacts remain

behavioral_verification.symptom: Bot responses appear garbled - users see text like '<|channel|>text\n...' or missing/corrupted content
behavioral_verification.required_observation: All bot responses display as clean, readable text without channel marker artifacts, with complete sentences and proper formatting
evidence_required: Response text contains no '<|channel' or '<channel|>' substrings
Response text is non-empty and matches expected model output length
Thinking content (when present) appears only in thinkingText field, not in main text

context_markdown:
## Agent Flow Context

Last agent: `planner`
Last phase: `plan`
Next agent requested by workflow: `implementer`

### Summary
Root cause: thoughtChannelRegex pattern does not match actual thinking channel marker format (expects slashes `</|channel|>` but actual markers use `<|channel>thought\n` and `<channel|>` without slashes). Since the regex never matches, the extractText() function passes through raw channel text unchanged. Additionally, extractText() uses channels["text"] directly without validating content, and the fallback message.toString() may include unparsed channel structure data. Fix: update regex to match actual marker format and add content validation.

### Problem Statement
```json
"Bot responses appear garbled/corrupted - users see mangled text that includes channel markers or missing content"
```

### Evidence
```json
[
  {
    "source": "LlmChatModelHelper.kt:27",
    "detail": "Regex '<\\|channel\\|>[\\s\\S]*?<\\|channel\\|>' expects opening '<|channel|>' and closing '</|channel|>' (with slashes)"
  },
  {
    "source": "MainActivity.kt:144-149",
    "detail": "getThinkingChannel() defines start='<|channel>thought\\n' and end='<channel|>' - NO slashes, different format entirely"
  },
  {
    "source": "LlmChatModelHelper.kt:34",
    "detail": "extractText() uses message.channels[\"text\"]?.toString() ?: message.toString() - if channels[\"text\"] is null or contains raw channel data, no validation occurs before display"
  },
  {
    "source": "LlmChatModelHelper.kt:343,414",
    "detail": "extractText(message) is called in both sendMessageAgentic and sendMessageSingleTurn on every onMessage callback - garbled text would propagate directly to UI"
  },
  {
    "source": "git diff LlmChatModelHelper.kt",
    "detail": "thoughtChannelRegex and extractText() were ADDED (not pre-existing) - the garbled output may be a regression from this change"
  }
]
```

### Acceptance Criteria
```json
[
  "Bot responses display clean readable text without channel marker artifacts",
  "Bot responses contain complete sentences and proper formatting",
  "Thinking content (when enabled) appears only in the thinking bubble, not in the main text response",
  "The extractText function returns non-null, non-empty string for valid messages"
]
```

### Files To Change
```json
[
  "/var/home/mateusoro/projetos/GEMMA4 ANDROID/app/src/main/java/com/gemma/gpuchat/LlmChatModelHelper.kt"
]
```

### Test Plan
```json
[
  "Build and install APK on device with NPU backend",
  "Send a simple message (e.g., 'Hello, how are you?') and observe response",
  "Send a message that triggers extended thinking and verify thinking content is separate from main text",
  "Send a PDF for processing and verify the response text is readable (not garbled)",
  "Check on GPU backend as well to ensure text extraction works for both configurations",
  "Log the raw channels[\"text\"] value before and after extractText() to verify no artifacts remain"
]
```

### Behavioral Verification
```json
{
  "symptom": "Bot responses appear garbled - users see text like '<|channel|>text\\n...' or missing/corrupted content",
  "required_observation": "All bot responses display as clean, readable text without channel marker artifacts, with complete sentences and proper formatting",
  "evidence_required": [
    "Response text contains no '<|channel' or '<channel|>' substrings",
    "Response text is non-empty and matches expected model output length",
    "Thinking content (when present) appears only in thinkingText field, not in main text"
  ]
}
```
